response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack web engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. Professional C++ experience is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments (2014-2015) and a bachelor's thesis project — far short of the 5-10 years required. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience (primarily MongoDB), and no fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge. A brief stint at RIDE Capital involved investment tooling but used Node.js/TypeScript, not C++, and did not involve capital markets systems.
strengths: Some C++ exposure at National Instruments writing components for NI LabWindows/CVI
CI/CD pipeline experience (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory) from Altom Consulting
Tangential financial-services exposure at RIDE Capital (investment platform, though non-C++ stack)
concerns: C++ professional experience is ~6 months vs. 5-10 years required — the single most critical gap
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is predominantly MongoDB and MySQL
